---
title: "Onboarding practice — participant-example"
synthetic: true
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Fictional example

`participant-example` is an invented alias. This page describes no real account, person, employer, partner, or project.
:::

## Practice identity

**Alias:** participant-example

**Fictional role:** Data documentation practice contributor

## Exercise completed

I completed the fictional Issue-to-pull-request onboarding exercise.

## Checklist

- [x] Located the assigned Issue
- [x] Read the objective and acceptance criteria
- [x] Claimed the Issue and posted a starting update
- [x] Created an Issue-linked branch
- [x] Opened the correct branch in GitHub Desktop
- [x] Reviewed the complete diff
- [x] Created a focused commit
- [x] Pushed the branch
- [x] Opened a linked pull request against `main`
- [x] Requested steward review
- [x] Responded to simulated review feedback

## Optional reflection

The most confusing fictional step was distinguishing a local commit from pushing the branch to GitHub.

This example demonstrates the expected safe structure only. It is not an actual participant record or pilot result.
